summary: ORD-CONSOLE-001 satisfies all 8 hard gates: every upstream requirement from TDD §5-§7/§9, ACF §3/§6, and DCF §5 has a verification item with concrete, timestamped, traceable evidence; no production-blocking open items remain; the one open item (structured startup log) is correctly classified as non-blocking.

warnings.description: §4.2 and §5.4 identify the same gap (structured startup log not emitted at process start) but are marked Not Verified rather than listed only in Open Items. The spec states 'Items not verified must be listed as open items' — the item IS listed in §9 Open Items, but both verification items remain in Not Verified status rather than being cross-referenced to the open item with a clear disposition. This is acceptable but could be cleaner.

ACF §5 (Reliability & Resilience Guardrails) requirements — availability, failure isolation, rollback — are covered substantively by §3.10, §5.5, §6.1-§6.10, and §8 but do not have dedicated verification items in a labeled ACF §5 section. The completeness rules reference 'ACF §5-§6' but the generation rules do not include an explicit mapping for ACF §5. Coverage is adequate through other sections.

§7.4 dependency audit evidence relies on reasoning about npm's --omit=dev behavior rather than a direct production-only audit scan. The conclusion (0 production CVEs) is correct — the Docker standalone build bundles only production deps — but a cleaner evidence path would be scanning the production Docker image directly.

§8 rollback procedure notes 'Full version-to-version rollback not tested (only one version exists).' This is an honest limitation but means rollback verification is partial — only restart-with-same-version was tested, not the TDD §9 procedure of running a previous image version.
